claim.description: A ZTL formula over named atoms, e.g. 'cleared & ~flagged'.

value.description: Kernel marking. T/F are decided verdicts; Z is the mark (unverified/unknown, default-deny). Fail-closed is FIRST-CLASS (SPEC §1): only 'T' or 'F' with admissible 'yes' pass as decided; any other string, null, or an absent value is coerced to Z. Dirty domain values are permitted here precisely so the connector can fail them closed rather than reject them.

provenance.description: Audit-only; the judge never reads it (SPEC §7).

admissible.description: 'no', or an absent/other value, is fail-closed to Z (SPEC §1). Only the exact string 'yes' admits a decided value.
